nodes.name: Theatre Awards
nodes.description: Tony Awards, Olivier Awards, and other theatre honors

items.prompt: Which 1949 Arthur Miller play about a failing traveling salesman and his fractured family won six Tony Awards including Best Play and the Pulitzer Prize for Drama?
items.explanation:
Won 6 of 6 Tony nominations in 1949: Best Play, Best Author, Best Director (Elia Kazan), Best Producer, Best Featured Actor (Arthur Kennedy), Best Scenic Design (Jo Mielziner)
Also won the 1949 Pulitzer Prize for Drama and New York Drama Critics' Circle Award
Lee J. Cobb originated Willy Loman; Mildred Dunnock played Linda, Cameron Mitchell played Happy
Opened February 10, 1949, at the Morosco Theatre; ran for 742 performances
The play follows Willy Loman, a 63-year-old salesman who retreats into memories as his career and family disintegrate
Considered one of the greatest American plays; has been revived on Broadway five times, winning three Tony Awards for Best Revival
Miller wrote the first act in a single day at a Connecticut studio he built himself
items.cardFront: 1949 Best Play — Arthur Miller, failing salesman, 6 Tonys

items.prompt: Which 1959 William Gibson play about a young teacher breaking through to a deaf and blind child in Alabama won the 1960 Tony Award for Best Play, with Anne Bancroft and Patty Duke in the lead roles?
items.explanation:
Won 4 Tony Awards in 1960: Best Play, Best Actress (Anne Bancroft), Best Direction (Arthur Penn), Best Stage Technician
Based on Helen Keller's autobiography *The Story of My Life*; adapted from Gibson's 1957 Playhouse 90 teleplay
Anne Bancroft starred as Annie Sullivan; Patty Duke, age 12, played Helen Keller
Opened October 19, 1959, at the Playhouse Theatre; ran for 719 performances
The 1962 film reunited Bancroft and Duke; both won Academy Awards (Best Actress and Best Supporting Actress)
Duke was one of the youngest performers ever cast in a major Broadway role
The famous water pump scene, where Helen connects the word "water" to the liquid, is one of the most iconic moments in American theatre
items.cardFront: 1960 Best Play — Gibson, Bancroft & Duke, deaf-blind child

items.prompt: Which 1961 Robert Bolt play about a Lord Chancellor who refuses to endorse his king's divorce won the 1962 Tony for Best Play, with Paul Scofield in the lead?
items.explanation:
Won the 1962 Tony Award for Best Play; Paul Scofield won Best Actor for his portrayal of Sir Thomas More
Written by Robert Bolt; tells the story of More's refusal to approve Henry VIII's divorce from Catherine of Aragon and remarriage to Anne Boleyn
Scofield originated the role in London at the Globe Theatre (now Gielgud) on July 1, 1960, before transferring to Broadway
Broadway opened November 22, 1961, at the ANTA Playhouse; ran for 620 performances
The 1966 film adaptation won six Oscars including Best Picture; Scofield reprised More and won the Academy Award for Best Actor
Scofield is one of roughly eleven actors to win both a Tony and Oscar for the same role
More was executed in 1535 and canonized as a Catholic saint in 1935; Bolt's play uses a narrator figure called the Common Man
items.cardFront: 1962 Best Play — Bolt, Scofield as Thomas More vs. Henry VIII

items.prompt: Which 1975 concept musical about 17 Broadway dancers auditioning for a chorus spot won 9 Tony Awards including Best Musical and held the record for longest-running Broadway show until 1997?
items.explanation:
Won 9 of 12 Tony nominations in 1976: Best Musical, Best Director/Choreographer (Michael Bennett), Best Book, Best Score (Marvin Hamlisch/Edward Kleban), Best Actress (Donna McKechnie), and others
Also won the 1976 Pulitzer Prize for Drama
Conceived, directed, and choreographed by Michael Bennett; built from tape-recorded interviews with real Broadway dancers
Opened July 25, 1975, at the Shubert Theatre; ran for 6,137 performances
Held the record for longest-running Broadway show until surpassed by *Cats* in 1997
Developed at Joseph Papp's Public Theater before transferring to Broadway
Signature gold-costumed kickline finale to "One" is among the most recognizable images in musical theatre

items.prompt: Which 1993 Tony Kushner two-part epic, subtitled 'A Gay Fantasia on National Themes,' won Best Play in both 1993 and 1994 for its two parts and explores AIDS in Reagan-era New York?
items.explanation:
Millennium Approaches won 4 Tonys in 1993 including Best Play; Perestroika won Best Play again in 1994
Millennium Approaches also won the 1993 Pulitzer Prize for Drama
Written by Tony Kushner; subtitled "A Gay Fantasia on National Themes"
Original cast: Stephen Spinella as Prior Walter, Ron Leibman as Roy Cohn, Joe Mantello as Louis, Jeffrey Wright as Belize, Marcia Gay Harden as Harper
Opened May 4, 1993, at the Walter Kerr Theatre; both parts ran in repertory
Set in 1985 New York; interweaves the stories of a gay man with AIDS, a closeted Mormon lawyer, and the real historical figure Roy Cohn
HBO produced a 2003 miniseries starring Al Pacino and Meryl Streep, directed by Mike Nichols; it won 11 Emmy Awards
items.cardFront: 1993 Best Play — Kushner, AIDS epic, won Best Play two years running

items.prompt: Which 2001 Mel Brooks musical comedy about two schemers who try to profit from a deliberately terrible Broadway show won a record 12 Tony Awards?
items.explanation:
Won 12 of 15 Tony nominations in 2001, breaking the record of 10 held by *Hello, Dolly!* since 1964
Awards included Best Musical, Best Book, Best Score (both Mel Brooks), Best Actor (Nathan Lane), Best Featured Actor (Gary Beach), Best Featured Actress (Cady Huffman), Best Director and Choreography (Susan Stroman)
Based on Brooks's 1967 film; book by Brooks and Thomas Meehan
Nathan Lane starred as Max Bialystock, Matthew Broderick as Leo Bloom; they originated the roles at the St. James Theatre
Opened April 19, 2001; ran for 2,502 performances
Broke the single-day box-office record at the time, taking in over $3 million
Brooks's Tony win for Best Score made him the eighth person to achieve EGOT status (Emmy, Grammy, Oscar, Tony)
items.cardFront: 2001 Best Musical — Brooks, record 12 Tonys, Lane & Broderick

items.prompt: Which 2005 John Patrick Shanley one-act play set in a 1964 Catholic school in the Bronx explores a nun's suspicion about a priest, winning Best Play and the Pulitzer Prize?
items.explanation:
Won the 2005 Tony Award for Best Play and the 2005 Pulitzer Prize for Drama
Written by John Patrick Shanley; full title is *Doubt: A Parable*
Cherry Jones won Best Actress as Sister Aloysius; Adriane Lenox won Best Featured Actress as Mrs. Muller
Original cast also included Brian F. O'Byrne as Father Flynn and Heather Goldenhersh as Sister James; directed by Doug Hughes
A 90-minute one-act with only four characters; premiered at Manhattan Theatre Club before transferring to the Walter Kerr Theatre
The 2008 film starred Meryl Streep and Philip Seymour Hoffman; earned five Oscar nominations
Shanley deliberately leaves the central question unresolved: the audience never learns whether the priest is guilty
items.cardFront: 2005 Best Play — Shanley, nun vs. priest, 1964 Bronx

items.prompt: Which 2006 Alan Bennett play about a group of Sheffield grammar school boys preparing for Oxbridge entrance exams won 6 Tony Awards including Best Play, with Richard Griffiths as an eccentric teacher?
items.explanation:
Won 6 of 7 Tony nominations in 2006: Best Play, Best Director (Nicholas Hytner), Best Actor (Richard Griffiths), Best Featured Actress (Frances de la Tour), and two design awards
Written by Alan Bennett; premiered at the National Theatre (Lyttelton) on May 18, 2004
Also won the 2005 Laurence Olivier Award for Best New Play before transferring to Broadway
Set in a 1980s Sheffield boys' grammar school; follows eight students preparing for Oxford and Cambridge entrance exams under three contrasting teachers
Richard Griffiths played Hector, a beloved but boundary-crossing English teacher
Original cast included future stars James Corden, Dominic Cooper, Russell Tovey, and Sacha Dhawan
A 2006 film adaptation retained the entire original cast and director Nicholas Hytner
items.cardFront: 2006 Best Play — Bennett, Sheffield boys, Oxbridge, Griffiths

items.prompt: Which 2008 Tracy Letts three-act family drama set in rural Oklahoma, about the explosive reunion of the dysfunctional Weston clan, won 5 Tonys including Best Play and the Pulitzer Prize?
items.explanation:
Won 5 Tony Awards in 2008: Best Play, Best Actress (Deanna Dunagan), Best Featured Actress (Rondi Reed), Best Director (Anna D. Shapiro), Best Scenic Design
Also won the 2008 Pulitzer Prize for Drama
Written by Tracy Letts, a member of Chicago's Steppenwolf Theatre Company where the play premiered on June 28, 2007
Set in a three-story house outside Pawhuska, Oklahoma; the patriarch Beverly Weston disappears, triggering a family reunion that unearths decades of secrets
A three-and-a-half-hour, 13-character epic blending tragedy with black comedy
Transferred to Broadway at the Imperial Theatre on December 4, 2007; ran for 648 performances
The 2013 film starred Meryl Streep as Violet and Julia Roberts as Barbara; both received Oscar nominations
items.cardFront: 2008 Best Play — Letts, Weston family, Oklahoma, 5 Tonys + Pulitzer

items.prompt: Which 2009 Elton John and Lee Hall musical about a coal miner's son who discovers ballet during the 1984 British miners' strike won 10 Tony Awards including Best Musical?
items.explanation:
Won 10 of 15 Tony nominations in 2009, including Best Musical, Best Book (Lee Hall), Best Direction (Stephen Daldry), Best Choreography (Peter Darling)
Music by Elton John, book and lyrics by Lee Hall; based on the 2000 Stephen Daldry film
Three boys (David Alvarez, Trent Kowalik, Kiril Kulish) shared the Tony for Best Actor, rotating in the title role
Set in County Durham, England, during the 1984–85 miners' strike; Billy's father and brother are striking miners while he secretly takes ballet classes
Gregory Jbara won Best Featured Actor as Billy's father Jackie
Opened on Broadway at the Imperial Theatre on November 13, 2008; ran until January 8, 2012
The London original premiered at the Victoria Palace Theatre in 2005 and ran for 11 years, closing in 2016
items.cardFront: 2009 Best Musical — Elton John, ballet + miners' strike, 10 Tonys

items.prompt: Which 2013 Christopher Durang comedy riffs on Chekhov while following three middle-aged siblings and a young boyfriend during a chaotic weekend in Bucks County, Pennsylvania, winning Best Play?
items.answer: Vanya and Sonia and Masha and Spike
alternates: Vanya and Sonia and Masha and Spike
items.explanation:
Won the 2013 Tony Award for Best Play from 6 nominations
Written by Christopher Durang; character names and themes drawn from Chekhov plays (*Uncle Vanya*, *Three Sisters*, *The Cherry Orchard*, *The Seagull*)
Starred David Hyde Pierce as Vanya, Sigourney Weaver as Masha, Kristine Nielsen as Sonia, Billy Magnussen as Spike
Set in present-day Bucks County, Pennsylvania; Vanya and Sonia stayed home to care for aging parents while Masha became a movie star
Opened March 14, 2013, at the John Golden Theatre; ran for 189 performances
Recouped its $2.75 million investment in under four months
Durang's biggest commercial hit; no familiarity with Chekhov required to enjoy the comedy, though every character and plot beat has a Chekhovian source
items.cardFront: 2013 Best Play — Durang, Chekhov homage, Weaver & Hyde Pierce
items.cardBack: Vanya and Sonia and Masha and Spike

items.prompt: Which 2015 Simon Stephens adaptation of a Mark Haddon novel follows a mathematically gifted 15-year-old who investigates a dog's murder, winning 5 Tonys including Best Play?
items.answer: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time
alternates: Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time
items.explanation:
Won 5 of 6 Tony nominations in 2015: Best Play, Best Actor (Alex Sharp), Best Director (Marianne Elliott), Best Lighting Design, Best Scenic Design
Adapted by Simon Stephens from Mark Haddon's 2003 novel; directed by Marianne Elliott
Alex Sharp won Best Actor in his first professional role, playing Christopher Boone, a 15-year-old on the autism spectrum
Premiere at the National Theatre's Cottesloe Theatre in London on August 2, 2012; tied the record for most Olivier Awards (7) including Best New Play in 2013
Broadway opened October 5, 2014, at the Ethel Barrymore Theatre; closed September 4, 2016
The production is known for its innovative staging using an LED-grid set to visualize Christopher's perception of the world
The title references a Sherlock Holmes quote from "Silver Blaze" about "the curious incident of the dog in the night-time"
items.cardFront: 2015 Best Play — Stephens/Haddon, autistic teen, 5 Tonys
items.cardBack: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time

items.prompt: Which 2016 Lin-Manuel Miranda hip-hop musical about a Founding Father received a record 16 Tony nominations and won 11, including Best Musical?
items.explanation:
Won 11 of a record 16 Tony nominations in 2016, the second-most wins ever after The Producers' 12
Awards included Best Musical, Best Score, Best Book (both Lin-Manuel Miranda), Best Actor (Leslie Odom Jr. as Aaron Burr), Best Featured Actor (Daveed Diggs as Lafayette/Jefferson)
Also won the 2016 Pulitzer Prize for Drama
Written by Lin-Manuel Miranda; tells the story of Alexander Hamilton using hip-hop, R&B, and traditional show tunes with a deliberately multiracial cast
Opened August 6, 2015, at the Richard Rodgers Theatre; Miranda originated the title role
Based on Ron Chernow's 2004 biography; Miranda conceived the idea while reading it on vacation in 2008
The Disney+ filmed version (2020) was captured with the original Broadway cast and became one of the platform's most-watched premieres
items.cardFront: 2016 Best Musical — Miranda, hip-hop Founding Father, record 16 nominations, 11 wins

items.prompt: Which 2017 musical about a socially anxious teenager whose fabricated connection to a classmate who died by suicide goes viral on social media won 6 Tonys including Best Musical?
items.explanation:
Won 6 of 9 Tony nominations in 2017: Best Musical, Best Book, Best Score (Benj Pasek and Justin Paul), Best Actor (Ben Platt), Best Featured Actress (Rachel Bay Jones), Best Orchestrations
Music and lyrics by Benj Pasek and Justin Paul; book by Steven Levenson
Ben Platt originated the title role; his performance of "Waving Through a Window" became a viral sensation
Opened at the Music Box Theatre in December 2016; closed September 18, 2022
The plot hinges on Evan fabricating a friendship with Connor Murphy after Connor's suicide, then being unable to confess as the lie spirals into a social media movement
Pasek and Paul also wrote the songs for *La La Land* (2016) and *The Greatest Showman* (2017), winning the Oscar for "City of Stars"
A 2021 film starred Platt reprising the role, generating controversy over his age (27) playing a high schooler
items.cardFront: 2017 Best Musical — Pasek & Paul, teen anxiety + social media, Platt

items.prompt: Which 2019 Anais Mitchell musical retelling the myth of Orpheus and Eurydice as a New Orleans-inflected folk opera won 8 Tony Awards including Best Musical?
items.explanation:
Won 8 of 14 Tony nominations in 2019: Best Musical, Best Original Score (Anais Mitchell), Best Direction (Rachel Chavkin), Best Scenic Design, Best Lighting Design, Best Sound Design, Best Orchestrations, Best Featured Actor
Book, music, and lyrics all by Anais Mitchell; the first Best Musical winner with book, music, and lyrics by a single woman
Retells the Greek myth of Orpheus and Eurydice alongside Hades and Persephone, blending folk, jazz, and New Orleans influences
Developed over seven years; started as a concept album (2010), then off-Broadway at New York Theatre Workshop (2016), then Broadway at the Walter Kerr Theatre (2019)
Rachel Chavkin won Best Director; in her acceptance speech she noted she was the only woman nominated in her category
Also won the 2020 Grammy Award for Best Musical Theater Album
The show's signature staging features a New Orleans speakeasy set with musicians visible onstage throughout
items.cardFront: 2019 Best Musical — Mitchell, Orpheus myth, folk-jazz, 8 Tonys
